message:
test(hook): strengthen UTF-8 truncation safety test with explicit assertions

Improves the test_colorful_warning_utf8_truncation_does_not_panic test
to provide better coverage and clearer failure messages.

Changes:
- Extended Chinese test string to >50 characters (triggers truncation)
- Extended Japanese test string to >50 characters
- Extended emoji test string to >50 characters
- Added explicit length assertions before each call:
  assert!(string.chars().count() > 50, "must be >50 chars, got {}")

This ensures the test actually exercises the truncation path rather than
potentially passing with strings that don't trigger truncation. The
explicit assertions make test failures more informative by showing the
actual character count.

The test verifies that print_colorful_warning() handles multi-byte UTF-8
characters safely when truncating long commands for display.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
